lenses.guidance: The plague pattern intensifies through repetition — request, refusal, sign, partial concession, hardening (vv 1-15, 16-19, 20-32). The narrative structure trains the reader to expect Pharaoh's relapse before it comes.

The Egyptian magicians' confession 'this is the finger of God' (v 19) echoes later at Lk 11:20, where Jesus casts out demons 'by the finger of God' — a canonical thread linking exodus power to Christ's exorcisms.

Pharaoh's 'pray for me' (v 8, v 28) followed by hardening (v 15, v 32) is the picture of partial repentance. Confess the sin you do not actually intend to leave, and you will find yourself returning to it. Repent fully or not at all.
